revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a 64x64 px pixel-art terrain tileset sprite sheet for game development. Style: SNES / 16-bit platformer world-map asset, crisp low-resolution pixels, no antialiasing blur, consistent bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, top-left light source, dark one-pixel outlines where useful. Canvas: exactly 64x64 px, strict 4 columns x 4 rows, 16 frames total, each frame exactly 16x16 px. No visible grid lines, no tile IDs, no labels, no borders, no text, no watermark. Every frame must stay inside its own 16x16 cell and use the same scale.

Subject: top-down-oblique mossy stacked-stone retaining-wall cliff tileset, viewed slightly from above and front, about 35 degrees, so the vertical stone face is clearly visible and conveys height. The wall is nozura-zumi irregular stacked mossy grey stone with lush deep matcha and moss-green grass on the plateau top, soft warm sunlight, bright cheerful colors. Use a solid #FF00FF magenta background only in any empty transparent-like space; otherwise the tile art may fill each cell edge to edge. Tileable horizontally and cohesive across rows.

Strict 4x4 layout:
Row 1, TOP RIM: frame 0 top-left outer corner, frame 1 straight top grassy plateau lip, frame 2 straight top edge variation with broken moss/grass overlap, frame 3 top-right outer corner.
Row 2, WALL FACE: frame 4 left end of vertical mossy stone face, frame 5 straight stacked-stone wall face, frame 6 straight wall face variation with different stone clusters and moss, frame 7 right end of wall face.
Row 3, BASE: frame 8 bottom-left corner where wall foot meets lower grass, frame 9 straight base with scattered stones and moss, frame 10 straight base variation with chipped stones and grass overlap, frame 11 bottom-right corner.
Row 4, STAIRS: frame 12 stair top where mossy stone steps meet plateau lip, frame 13 middle descending steps toward the viewer, frame 14 stair bottom where steps meet lower ground, frame 15 extra mossy stone face accent tile.

Tilemap quality: avoid checkerboard feel and repeated square stamps; vary stone shapes, moss clusters, edge breakup, and light while keeping the set cohesive. Include usable center/edge/corner/inner-corner/chipped/grass-overlap/decoration variants through the described frames. The transition between grass, stone, moss, and lower ground must look natural, not rectangular bands.

Avoid completely: any characters, creatures, birds, companion animals, samurai armor, kabuto helmet, ninja outfit, two-sword daisho, guns, modern objects, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, logos, likenesses, letter emblems, UI, text, watermark, and any recognizable Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna elements.
